channel.title: Sharp Line Cuts, Shop Journal
channel.description: Notes from the chair on Livernois. Shop news, neighborhood, and the road to the second location.

item.title: Twelve Years, Same Corner
item.description: We opened in 2014 with one chair and a six-month lease. Twelve years later there are three barbers, an apprentice, and a waiting bench that fills up on Saturdays. Thank you to the neighborhood that kept the lights on.

item.title: Meet Ty, Our Apprentice
item.description: Ty grew up three blocks from the shop and started his apprenticeship in 2025. He is quiet, focused, and learning fast. If you sit with him on an apprentice rate, you are part of how a barber gets made.

item.title: Cuts Before Christmas Is Back
item.description: Our holiday community day returns to the Livernois shop. Free kids cuts, food from El Sol Kitchen, music, and product giveaways while supplies last. All hands on deck. Bring the family.

item.title: Why We Are Opening on McNichols
item.description: The McNichols corridor is growing and quality chairs are scarce. We are taking the Sharp Line standard a few miles north with a soft opening targeted for spring 2027. Same fades, more seats.

item.title: The Playlist Is the Point
item.description: The shop has a sound for a reason. Hip-hop, Tejano, cumbia, a little Latin soul, and worship on Sunday mornings. James will change a track mid-song if the energy is wrong. That is not pickiness, that is hospitality.
